cultural.en: relating to the ideas, customs, and social behavior of a society

ex.en: Korea has a rich cultural heritage.

cure.en: make someone healthy again by treating an illness

ex.en: Doctors are working to cure the disease.
ex.ko: 의사들은 그 병을 치료하기 위해 노력하고 있다.

cure.en: a medicine or treatment that makes an illness go away

ex.en: Scientists are searching for a cure for cancer.

curiosity.en: a strong desire to know or learn something

ex.en: Her curiosity led her to explore the old house.
ex.ko: 그녀의 호기심이 그녀를 그 오래된 집을 탐험하게 만들었다.

curious.en: eager to know or learn about something

ex.en: The children were curious about the strange noise.
ex.ko: 아이들은 그 이상한 소리에 대해 궁금해했다.

ex.en: It was a curious coincidence that they met again.
ex.ko: 그들이 다시 만난 것은 기묘한 우연이었다.

curiously.en: in a way that shows interest in learning about something

ex.en: She looked curiously at the strange device.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 이상한 기기를 호기심 어린 눈으로 바라보았다.

curiously.en: used to say that something is strange or surprising

ex.en: Curiously, no one noticed the mistake.
ex.ko: 신기하게도, 아무도 그 실수를 알아차리지 못했다.

ex.en: She has curly brown hair.

currency.en: the money used in a particular country

ex.en: The local currency is the won.

currency.en: the state of being generally accepted or used

ex.en: The rumor quickly gained currency online.
She is currently studying in London.

curriculum.en: all the subjects taught in a school or course

ex.en: Music was added to the school curriculum.

curtain.en: a piece of cloth hung to cover a window or divide a room

ex.en: She closed the curtains before going to bed.

curve.en: a line that bends smoothly without any sharp angles

ex.en: The road has a sharp curve ahead.
ex.ko: 그 도로는 앞쪽에 급격한 커브가 있다.

curve.en: bend in a smooth, gradual way

ex.en: The path curves around the lake.

cushion.en: a soft cloth bag filled with feathers or foam, used for comfort

ex.en: She placed a cushion on the chair.

cushion.en: reduce the bad effects of a shock or impact

ex.en: The savings will cushion the family against job loss.
ex.ko: 그 저축은 그 가족이 실직으로 인한 타격을 완화해 줄 것이다.

customs.en: the place at a border where officials check goods and luggage

ex.en: We went through customs at the airport.

CV.en: a document listing your education and work experience

ex.en: Please send your CV and cover letter.

cycle.en: a series of events that happen again and again in the same order

ex.en: The water cycle includes evaporation and rainfall.
He rides his cycle to work every day.
They cycled along the river path.

daisy.en: a small flower with white petals and a yellow center

ex.en: She picked daisies in the field.

damage.en: physical harm that makes something worse or breaks it

ex.en: The storm caused a lot of damage to the roof.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍은 지붕에 많은 피해를 입혔다.

damage.en: cause harm to something so it is broken or in worse condition

ex.en: Smoking can damage your lungs.
The package arrived damaged.
The towel felt damp to the touch.

dangerously.en: in a way that could cause harm or danger

ex.en: He was driving dangerously on the icy road.

dare.en: have enough courage to do something risky

ex.en: He didn't dare to speak in front of the class.
ex.ko: 그는 감히 반 친구들 앞에서 말하지 못했다.

dare.en: challenge someone to do something difficult or risky

ex.en: She dared him to jump into the cold water.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그에게 차가운 물에 뛰어들어 보라고 부추겼다.

darkness.en: the state of having no light

ex.en: The room was covered in darkness.

dating.en: the activity of going out with someone you are romantically interested in

ex.en: Online dating has become very popular.

daze.en: a state of being confused and unable to think clearly, often after a shock

ex.en: She walked around in a daze after the accident.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사고 후 멍한 상태로 돌아다녔다.

dazzle.en: make someone unable to see well because of very bright light

ex.en: The headlights dazzled the driver.
ex.ko: 그 헤드라이트가 운전자의 눈을 부시게 했다.

dazzle.en: impress someone greatly with skill, beauty, or intelligence

ex.en: Her performance dazzled the audience.

deadline.en: the time by which something must be finished

ex.en: The deadline for the project is Friday.
The snake's bite can be deadly.
ex.ko: 그 뱀에 물리면 치명적일 수 있다.

deaf.en: unable to hear, wholly or in part

ex.en: He has been deaf since birth.
ex.ko: 그는 태어날 때부터 청각 장애가 있다.

deaf.ko: 들으려 하지 않는, 귀 기울이지 않는
deaf.en: unwilling to listen to or consider something

ex.en: She was deaf to all our warnings.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우리의 모든 경고를 들으려 하지 않았다.

dealer.en: a person or business that buys and sells a particular type of goods

ex.en: He bought his car from a local dealer.
The police arrested a drug dealer downtown.

debris.en: pieces of something that has been destroyed or broken

ex.en: Workers cleared the debris after the storm.

debt.en: an amount of money that you owe someone

ex.en: He is still paying off his student debt.
ex.ko: 그는 아직 학자금 빚을 갚고 있다.

decision.en: a choice that you make after thinking about it

ex.en: She made the right decision to quit her job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 일을 그만두는 옳은 결정을 내렸다.

ex.en: The country issued a declaration of independence.

declaration.en: an official statement of goods you are bringing into a country

ex.en: Fill out the customs declaration before you land.
The government declared a state of emergency.

declare.en: tell customs officials about goods you are bringing into a country

ex.en: Do you have anything to declare?

decline.en: politely refuse an offer or invitation

ex.en: He declined the invitation to the party.

decline.en: become gradually worse, smaller, or weaker

ex.en: Sales have declined sharply this year.

decline.en: a gradual decrease in quality, quantity, or strength

ex.en: The company is experiencing a decline in profits.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 수익 감소를 겪고 있다.

decrease.en: become smaller in size, number, or amount

ex.en: The population decreased over the past decade.

decrease.en: a reduction in size, number, or amount

ex.en: There was a decrease in crime rates last year.

dedicate.en: give all your time or effort to a particular activity or purpose

ex.en: She dedicated her life to helping others.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다른 사람들을 돕는 데 자신의 삶을 바쳤다.

dedicate.en: say that something you have created is in honor of someone

ex.en: He dedicated the song to his late mother.
ex.ko: 그는 그 노래를 돌아가신 어머니께 헌정했다.

deed.en: something that someone does, especially a brave or important act

ex.en: He was known for his heroic deeds.

deed.en: a legal document showing who owns a property

ex.en: She signed the deed to the house.

defeat.en: win a victory over someone in a fight, game, or contest

ex.en: Our team defeated the champions in the final.

defeat.en: a failure to win a fight, game, or contest

ex.en: The team suffered a heavy defeat.

defend.en: protect someone or something from attack or harm

ex.en: The soldiers defended the city against the invaders.

defend.en: speak or argue in support of someone accused in court

ex.en: The lawyer defended her client in court.

defender.en: a player whose job is to stop the other team from scoring

ex.en: He plays as a defender for his soccer team.

defender.en: a person who protects or supports someone or something

ex.en: She is a strong defender of human rights.

defense.en: the act of protecting someone or something from attack, or a country's military protection

ex.en: The country increased its defense spending this year.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 올해 국방 예산을 늘렸다.

defense.en: the players on a sports team who try to stop the other team from scoring

ex.en: Our defense played very well in the second half.

deficiency.en: a lack of something that is needed

ex.en: A vitamin deficiency can cause health problems.
ex.ko: 비타민 결핍은 건강 문제를 일으킬 수 있다.

define.en: explain exactly what a word or idea means

ex.en: Can you define this word for me?
ex.ko: 이 단어를 정의해 줄 수 있어?

define.en: show or describe clearly what the nature or limits of something are

ex.en: Honesty defines her character.

definite.en: clear, certain, and not likely to change

ex.en: We need a definite answer by tomorrow.
ex.ko: 우리는 내일까지 확실한 답을 받아야 한다.

definite article.en: the word 'the', used before a noun to refer to a specific thing

ex.en: 'The' is the definite article in English.
I will definitely come to the party.

definition.en: a statement that explains the meaning of a word or phrase

ex.en: Look up the definition of this word in the dictionary.

delete.en: remove something, especially text or data on a computer

ex.en: She deleted the file by mistake.
The necklace has a delicate design.
ex.ko: 그 목걸이는 섬세한 디자인을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: Handle the glass vase carefully; it's delicate.
ex.ko: 그 유리 꽃병은 연약하니 조심히 다뤄.

ex.en: They discussed a delicate topic during the meeting.

delight.en: a feeling of great pleasure or happiness

ex.en: She squealed with delight when she saw the puppy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 강아지를 보고 기뻐서 소리를 질렀다.

ex.en: The surprise party delighted her.
ex.ko: 그 깜짝 파티는 그녀를 기쁘게 했다.

ex.en: I'm delighted to meet you.
We had a delightful evening together.
ex.ko: 우리는 함께 매우 즐거운 저녁을 보냈다.

delivery.en: the act of bringing goods or letters to a place

ex.en: The delivery arrived an hour late.

delivery.en: the process of giving birth to a baby

ex.en: She had a difficult delivery.

demand.en: a strong or urgent request for something

ex.en: The workers made a demand for higher wages.

demand.en: the amount of a product that people want to buy

ex.en: There is high demand for electric cars.

demand.en: ask for something firmly and urgently

ex.en: She demanded an apology from him.

democracy.en: a system of government in which people vote to choose their leaders

ex.en: South Korea is a democracy.

democratic.en: based on the principles of democracy

ex.en: They held a democratic election.

demonstrate.en: show how to do something or how something works

ex.en: The teacher demonstrated the experiment to the class.
ex.ko: 선생님은 학급에게 그 실험을 시연해 보였다.

demonstrate.en: prove something is true by giving evidence

ex.en: The study demonstrates a clear link between diet and health.
ex.ko: 그 연구는 식습관과 건강 사이의 명확한 연관성을 입증한다.

demonstrate.en: take part in a public protest

ex.en: Thousands demonstrated against the new law.
ex.ko: 수천 명이 새 법에 반대하여 시위했다.

demonstration.en: an act of showing how something works or is done

ex.en: He gave a cooking demonstration at the fair.

demonstration.en: a public gathering to protest about something

ex.en: A large demonstration took place downtown.

deny.en: say that something is not true

ex.en: He denied stealing the money.
ex.ko: 그는 그 돈을 훔쳤다는 것을 부인했다.

deny.en: refuse to allow someone to have or do something

ex.en: She was denied entry to the club.

depart.en: leave a place, especially to start a journey

ex.en: The train departs at 9 a.m.

department.en: a specific section of an organization, company, or university

ex.en: She works in the marketing department.

departure.en: the act of leaving a place

ex.en: Our departure was delayed due to bad weather.

dependent.en: relying on someone or something else for help or support

ex.en: Children are dependent on their parents.

dependent.en: a person who relies on someone else, especially financially

ex.en: He has three dependents to support.
ex.ko: 그는 부양해야 할 가족이 세 명 있다.

deposit.en: put money into a bank account

ex.en: She deposited her paycheck at the bank.

deposit.en: an amount of money paid in advance as a guarantee

ex.en: We paid a deposit to reserve the apartment.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 아파트를 예약하기 위해 보증금을 냈다.

deposit.en: money placed in a bank account

ex.en: He made a large deposit into his savings account.
ex.ko: 그는 저축 계좌에 큰 금액을 예금했다.

depressed.en: feeling very sad and without hope

ex.en: He has felt depressed since losing his job.

depressing.en: making you feel sad or without hope

ex.en: The news was really depressing.

depression.en: a mental illness causing continuous sad feelings and loss of interest

ex.en: She has been treated for depression for years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 몇 년간 우울증 치료를 받아왔다.

depression.en: a long period of very low economic activity and high unemployment

ex.en: The country suffered a severe economic depression.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 심각한 경제 불황을 겪었다.

deprive.en: prevent someone from having or enjoying something

ex.en: The prisoners were deprived of sleep.

depth.en: how deep something is, from top to bottom

ex.en: The pool has a depth of two meters.

depth.en: the quality of being detailed, serious, or thorough

ex.en: His speech showed great depth of understanding.
The word 'biology' derives from Greek.

derive.en: get something, especially a feeling or benefit, from a particular source

ex.en: She derives great satisfaction from her work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 일에서 큰 만족을 얻는다.

deserve.en: be worthy of or entitled to something because of your actions or qualities

ex.en: She deserves a promotion for her hard work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열심히 일한 대가로 승진할 자격이 있다.

designer.en: a person whose job is to plan how something will look or work

ex.en: She works as a fashion designer.

designer.en: made by a famous designer and therefore expensive

ex.en: He was wearing designer jeans.
She desires a quiet life in the countryside.

desire.en: a strong feeling of wanting something

ex.en: He had a strong desire to travel the world.
ex.ko: 그는 세계를 여행하고 싶은 강한 욕구가 있었다.

despair.en: a feeling of complete loss of hope

ex.en: She was filled with despair after losing her job.

desperate.en: feeling that you must have or do something very much, often because a situation is very serious

ex.en: He was desperate to find a job.

despite.en: without being affected by something; even though

ex.en: Despite the rain, they went hiking.
ex.ko: 비가 오는데도 불구하고 그들은 하이킹을 갔다.

destination.en: the place you are traveling to

ex.en: Our final destination was Paris.

destruction.en: the act of destroying something completely

ex.en: The war caused massive destruction.
The hurricane was extremely destructive.

detective.en: a person, often a police officer, whose job is to investigate crimes

ex.en: The detective solved the case quickly.

determination.en: a firm decision to do something despite difficulty

ex.en: She showed great determination to succeed.

determine.en: find out something by investigation or calculation

ex.en: Scientists determined the cause of the illness.
Your effort will determine your success.

devastate.en: destroy a place or thing completely

ex.en: The earthquake devastated the entire village.
The news of his death devastated the family.
ex.ko: 그의 죽음 소식은 가족에게 큰 충격을 주었다.

ex.en: The flood had a devastating effect on the town.
ex.ko: 그 홍수는 마을에 파괴적인 영향을 미쳤다.

devastating.en: causing great shock or emotional distress

ex.en: It was devastating news for everyone.

development.en: the process of growing, improving, or being created

ex.en: The company invests heavily in software development.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 소프트웨어 개발에 많은 투자를 한다.

development.en: a new event or piece of information that affects a situation

ex.en: There have been new developments in the investigation.

device.en: a machine or tool made for a particular purpose

ex.en: This device measures your heart rate.

devotion.en: great love or loyalty for a person, activity, or cause

ex.en: Her devotion to her family is admirable.

diagram.en: a drawing that explains how something works

ex.en: The diagram shows how the engine works.
ex.ko: 그 도표는 엔진이 어떻게 작동하는지 보여준다.

dial.en: the round face of a clock, radio, or other device that shows numbers or settings

ex.en: He turned the dial to change the channel.

dial.en: press or turn the numbers on a phone to call someone

ex.en: She dialed his number twice.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 번호를 두 번 눌렀다.

dialog.en: a conversation between two or more people

ex.en: The book is full of natural dialog.

diameter.en: the length of a straight line through the center of a circle

ex.en: The circle has a diameter of ten centimeters.

diaper.en: a piece of soft material worn by babies to absorb waste

ex.en: She changed the baby's diaper.

differ.en: be different from something or someone else

ex.en: Their opinions differ on this issue.

differ.en: have a different opinion from someone

ex.en: I differ with you on that point.
ex.ko: 나는 그 점에서 너와 의견이 다르다.

digital.en: using electronic technology that records information as numbers, rather than analog

ex.en: She took the photo with a digital camera.

diligence.en: careful and steady effort in doing something

ex.en: He completed the project with great diligence.
ex.ko: 그는 큰 근면함으로 그 프로젝트를 완수했다.

ex.en: She is a diligent student who studies every day.

dioxide.cue: 산소 원자 두 개가 결합한 화합물
dioxide.en: a chemical compound containing two atoms of oxygen

ex.en: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air.

directly.en: in a direct way, without anyone or anything in between

ex.en: You can contact the manager directly.
She left directly after the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의가 끝난 직후 곧바로 떠났다.

dirt.en: soil or a substance that makes things unclean

ex.en: His shoes were covered in dirt.
The magazine dug up some dirt on the celebrity.
ex.ko: 그 잡지는 그 유명인에 대한 좋지 않은 소문을 캐냈다.

disability.en: a physical or mental condition that limits a person's movements, senses, or activities

ex.en: The building has ramps for people with disabilities.
ex.ko: 그 건물에는 장애가 있는 사람들을 위한 경사로가 있다.

disable.en: make something unable to work or function

ex.en: The virus disabled the security system.

disabled.en: having a physical or mental condition that limits movement or activity

ex.en: The parking spot is reserved for disabled drivers.
ex.ko: 그 주차 공간은 장애가 있는 운전자를 위해 예약되어 있다.

disagreement.en: a situation in which people have different opinions

ex.en: They had a disagreement about the schedule.
ex.ko: 그들은 일정에 대해 의견 차이가 있었다.

disappoint.en: fail to meet someone's hopes or expectations

ex.en: The movie disappointed many fans.

disappointment.en: the feeling of being unhappy because something did not happen as expected

ex.en: She couldn't hide her disappointment.

disaster.en: a sudden event that causes great damage or suffering

ex.en: The flood was a natural disaster.

disastrous.en: causing great and often sudden damage or harm

ex.en: The decision had disastrous consequences.

disc jockey.en: a person who plays recorded music for an audience, on the radio or at a club

ex.en: The disc jockey played our favorite song.
ex.ko: 그 디제이는 우리가 좋아하는 노래를 틀어주었다.

discomfort.en: a feeling of slight pain or being uncomfortable, physically or mentally

ex.en: He felt some discomfort in his back.

discount.en: a reduction in the usual price of something

ex.en: I got a 20% discount on the shoes.
The store discounted all winter clothes.

discourage.en: make someone lose confidence or enthusiasm

ex.en: Don't let one failure discourage you.

discourage.en: try to persuade someone not to do something

ex.en: Her parents discouraged her from quitting school.
ex.ko: 그녀의 부모님은 그녀가 학교를 그만두는 것을 말렸다.

discovery.en: the act of finding something that was not known before

ex.en: The discovery of penicillin changed medicine forever.

discrimination.en: unfair treatment of people because of who they are

ex.en: The law protects workers from discrimination.

disease.en: an illness that affects a person, animal, or plant

ex.en: Doctors are researching a cure for the disease.
ex.ko: 의사들은 그 질병의 치료법을 연구하고 있다.

disgusting.en: extremely unpleasant, causing a strong feeling of dislike

ex.en: The smell in the kitchen was disgusting.
Put the dirty plates in the dishwasher.
The coach threw the disk across the field.

disk.en: a flat device used to store computer information

ex.en: She saved the file onto a disk.

dissolve.en: become mixed into a liquid completely

ex.en: Sugar dissolves quickly in hot water.

dissolve.en: come to an end, or officially bring something to an end

ex.en: Parliament was dissolved before the election.

distance.en: the amount of space between two places or things

ex.en: The distance between the two cities is 300 kilometers.

distance.en: a place that is far away

ex.en: They could see mountains in the distance.

distant.en: far away in space or time

ex.en: They live in a distant country.

distant.en: not friendly or warm in manner

ex.en: He seemed distant during the conversation.
There is a clear distinction between the two ideas.
ex.ko: 그 두 생각 사이에는 명확한 차이가 있다.

distinction.en: a special honor or recognition for excellence

ex.en: She graduated with distinction.

distinctly.en: in a way that is clear and easy to notice

ex.en: I distinctly remember locking the door.
ex.ko: 나는 문을 잠갔다는 것을 뚜렷하게 기억한다.

distinguish.en: recognize the difference between two things

ex.en: It's hard to distinguish the twins.

distribute.en: give a share of something to a number of people

ex.en: The teacher distributed the worksheets to the students.

distribute.en: supply goods to shops or businesses over a wide area

ex.en: The company distributes its products worldwide.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 전 세계에 제품을 유통한다.

distribution.en: the way something is shared or given out among people

ex.en: The charity is responsible for the distribution of food to families in need.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 어려운 가정에 식량을 분배하는 일을 담당한다.

distribution.en: the business of supplying goods to shops and other businesses

ex.en: The company handles distribution for several brands.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 여러 브랜드의 유통을 담당한다.

district.en: an area of a town or country that has a particular feature or purpose

ex.en: He works in the financial district.

disturbance.en: an event that disrupts a calm or peaceful situation

ex.en: The neighbors caused a disturbance late at night.
He dove into the pool from the diving board.
Stock prices dived after the announcement.

dive.en: an act of diving into water

ex.en: Her dive earned a perfect score.

diver.en: a person who swims underwater, often with special equipment

ex.en: The diver explored the coral reef.
The city has a diverse population.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 다양한 인구를 가지고 있다.

divine.en: relating to or coming from God or a god

ex.en: They believed the king had divine authority.
ex.ko: 그들은 왕이 신성한 권위를 가졌다고 믿었다.

diving.en: the sport or activity of jumping into water, often from a board

ex.en: She practices diving every weekend.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매 주말 다이빙 연습을 한다.

ex.en: My parents got divorced when I was young.

DJ.en: a person who plays music for people to listen or dance to

ex.en: The DJ kept the party going all night.
ex.ko: 그 디제이는 밤새 파티 분위기를 이어갔다.

doc.en: an informal word for a doctor

ex.en: Hey doc, when will I feel better?

doc.en: an informal short form of 'document', a file with text

ex.en: Can you send me that Word doc?

document.en: a piece of written, printed, or electronic material that provides information

ex.en: Please sign this document.

documentary.en: a film or TV program that gives facts about real people or events

ex.en: We watched a documentary about climate change.
ex.ko: 우리는 기후 변화에 관한 다큐멘터리를 봤다.

dolphin.en: a small intelligent sea mammal with a curved back and pointed snout

ex.en: Dolphins are known for their intelligence.
There is a small dot at the end of the sentence.
ex.ko: 그 문장 끝에는 작은 점이 있다.

downward.en: moving or leading toward a lower place or level

ex.en: The company is on a downward trend.

doze.en: sleep lightly for a short time

ex.en: He dozed off during the meeting.
She had a quick doze on the couch.

dozen.en: a group of twelve of something

ex.en: She bought a dozen eggs.

drag.en: pull something heavy along the ground with effort

ex.en: He dragged the suitcase across the floor.
ex.ko: 그는 여행 가방을 바닥에 끌고 갔다.

drag.en: move something on a computer screen by clicking and moving the mouse

ex.en: Drag the file into the folder.

drag.en: continue for longer than necessary, becoming boring

ex.en: The meeting dragged on for hours.
ex.ko: 그 회의는 몇 시간이나 질질 끌었다.

dramatic.en: sudden and striking, causing surprise or excitement

ex.en: There was a dramatic increase in sales.

dramatic.en: relating to plays or the theater

ex.en: She studied dramatic arts in college.

drown.en: die from being underwater and unable to breathe

ex.en: The swimmer nearly drowned in the strong current.
ex.ko: 그 수영하던 사람은 거센 물살에 거의 익사할 뻔했다.

drown.en: make a sound impossible to hear by being much louder

ex.en: The music drowned out his voice.
ex.ko: 그 음악이 그의 목소리를 압도해 버렸다.

drunk.en: affected by alcohol so that you cannot think or behave normally

ex.en: He was too drunk to drive home.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 취해서 운전해서 집에 갈 수 없었다.

drunk.en: a person who is drunk or drinks alcohol regularly

ex.en: A drunk was shouting outside the bar.
ex.ko: 술 취한 사람이 술집 밖에서 소리를 지르고 있었다.

dude.en: an informal word for a man (used especially by young people)

ex.en: Hey dude, how's it going?
The lecture was so dull that I almost fell asleep.
ex.ko: 그 강의는 너무 지루해서 나는 거의 잠들 뻔했다.

dull.en: not sharp; not easy to cut with

ex.en: This knife is too dull to cut the bread.
ex.ko: 이 칼은 너무 무뎌서 빵을 자를 수 없다.

ex.en: The sky was a dull gray all day.

dump.en: get rid of something you don't want, often carelessly

ex.en: He dumped his old furniture on the street.

dump.en: a place where waste materials are left

ex.en: They took the broken appliances to the dump.
ex.ko: 그들은 고장 난 가전제품을 쓰레기장으로 가져갔다.
